nativeRange.habitat: Steep outer reef slopes at depths generally 30–100 m; adults tend to favor rubble and sandy areas near drop-offs adjacent to deep water, often associated with sponges and soft corals.

diet.notes: Primarily feeds on small meaty items including vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, chopped crustaceans, and small invertebrates. Has been reported to accept high-quality marine flake food. Established adults have been known to prey on small ornamental shrimp.

summary: A small, strikingly colored reef fish with bright yellow juveniles that transition through orange to pink with maturity. Generally considered peaceful and reef-safe with fish, though established adults may take interest in small ornamental shrimp.
careNotes:
The Yellow Candy Hogfish (Bodianus bimaculatus) is generally considered a hardy and moderately easy-to-keep species that tends to adapt well to captivity. Juveniles are bright yellow and notably vivid; females develop orange hues while mature males display predominantly pink coloration with red lines — making this species a notable example of sequential color phase changes within the genus Bodianus (protogynous hermaphrodite, as is typical of wrasses and hogfish).

In the wild, adults have been observed forming small aggregations in soft-bottom habitats with sponges and soft corals at depths of 30–100 m — somewhat deeper than many other popular reef fish. In aquaria, they generally do well in reef tanks and have been described as a very peaceful fish that adjusts quickly. However, multiple individuals kept together can exhibit mild territorial behavior; simultaneous introduction is generally advised to reduce this. Established adults have been known to prey on smaller ornamental shrimp introduced after the hogfish is settled.

Adequate hiding places in live rock are generally recommended, along with open swimming space.

Size cross-source note: FishBase records a maximum of 5.9 cm SL (standard length), which equates to roughly 7 cm total length. LiveAquaria lists a maximum of 4 inches (approx. 10 cm TL). The adultSizeCm range of 4–10 represents the spread across both sources; actual aquarium specimens are typically in the 6–10 cm range.

Temperature cross-source note: LiveAquaria gives 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); FishBase gives a wild preferred range of 22–27°C (mean 27.3°C). The filed range of 22–26°C represents a reasonable average across sources.
breedingNotes: Reported oviparous with distinct pairing during breeding, consistent with other Labridae. As a protogynous hermaphrodite, females may transition to male in the absence of a dominant male. Captive breeding has not been reliably documented for this species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page: minimum tank 30 gal, temperature 72–78°F, SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, adult size 4 in, care level moderate, reef safe yes, peaceful temperament.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (family Labridae, order Eupercaria/misc), adult size (5.9 cm SL ≈ 7 cm TL), native range (Madagascar to New Caledonia, Japan to New Zealand), habitat (steep outer reef slopes, 30–100 m depth), temperature range (22–27°C preferred). Size and temperature differ from LiveAquaria; values averaged across sources. FishBase common name for this species is 'Twospot Hogfish'; 'Yellow Candy Hogfish' is the aquarium trade name. Social behavior: forms small aggregations with one dominant male over several females (haremic), confirming protogynous pattern.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing during breeding. Captive breeding not reliably documented.
